title: 🔴LIVE - Full AI Video Generation Workflow Using Claude Code + Remotion + Archon
summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a production-grade AI video generation workflow that takes a URL as input and produces a fully rendered MP4. The system uses Claude for planning and code generation, Remotion for video composition, Archon for orchestration, and Cartesia and ElevenLabs for voice, music, and sound effects.
writeup: This livestream walks through a fully agentic video-production pipeline built on Archon, ElevenLabs (or Cartesia) for voice synthesis, and Remotion for programmatic video rendering. A single YAML workflow file orchestrates every stage: article fetch, narration authoring, sound-effect generation, voice cloning, and Remotion assembly, taking roughly 15 minutes to produce a short-form video from one prompt. The through-line is Archon's core design principle: encode the process as a durable, versioned asset rather than stuffing instructions into agent context, making large agentic workflows reliable and token-efficient.
key_points: Archon is an open-source harness builder that encodes agentic processes as YAML workflow files, turning a multi-step pipeline into a repeatable, versioned asset rather than a fragile set of markdown instructions fed to an agent.

The video-generation workflow chains article ingestion, narration writing, sound-effect creation, ElevenLabs or Cartesia voice synthesis, and Remotion video rendering inside one YAML file, invoked by telling Claude Code (or Codex) to generate a video on a topic.

Archon's key architectural insight is to build agents into workflows rather than workflows into agents: deterministic steps that must always run (validation, command sequences) are enforced by the harness, not left to the model's memory.

The YAML format supports looping, per-step model and provider selection (e.g., Claude for implementation and Codex for validation), parallel workflow execution via worktrees, and an interactive mode requiring human approval before the agent proceeds.

First-pass output from the demo (a TI-84 Evo calculator explainer) contained voice blips but required zero iteration, and the workflow supports iterative refinement by feeding Claude Code feedback on the generated video within the same session.

Setup requires only a Cartesia or ElevenLabs API key plus an Archon installation; loading the Archon skill file into the dot-Claude skills directory gives any Claude Code session immediate knowledge of how to invoke the workflow.
